title: oenothera fruticosa subsp. glauca
intro: Oenothera fruticosa subsp. glauca, also known as the glaucous evening primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The glaucous evening primrose has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting liver function. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its potential benefits.
